era_true.statement: Bilbo Baggins found the One Ring in the tunnels beneath the Misty Mountains.
Gollum possessed the One Ring for nearly five hundred years before losing it.
Smaug the dragon occupied the Lonely Mountain after driving out the dwarves of Erebor.
Gandalf is one of the five Istari sent to Middle-earth.
Frodo Baggins carried the One Ring from the Shire to Mount Doom.
Aragorn is the heir of Isildur and rightful King of Gondor.
The Fellowship of the Ring consisted of nine members.
Boromir died defending Merry and Pippin from orcs.
Legolas is an Elf of the Woodland Realm and son of Thranduil.
Gimli is the son of Glóin, one of Thorin's companions.
Samwise Gamgee accompanied Frodo into Mordor and carried him up Mount Doom.
The Shire is located in the northwest of Middle-earth in the region called Eriador.
Rivendell is an Elven refuge ruled by Elrond Half-elven.
Saruman the White betrayed the order of wizards and allied with Sauron's cause.
Isengard was Saruman's stronghold, centered on the tower of Orthanc.
The Ents attacked Isengard and destroyed Saruman's war machinery.
Treebeard is the oldest of the Ents.
Merry and Pippin were captured by Uruk-hai after the breaking of the Fellowship.
Gandalf fell with the Balrog in the Mines of Moria.
Gandalf returned from death as Gandalf the White.
The Balrog that Gandalf fought dwelt in the depths of Khazad-dûm.
Théoden was King of Rohan during the War of the Ring.
Éowyn slew the Witch-king of Angmar on the Pelennor Fields.
Éowyn disguised herself as a male rider named Dernhelm to ride to battle.
Merry stabbed the Witch-king with a Barrow-blade, breaking his power.
The Dead Men of Dunharrow were summoned by Aragorn using his authority as Isildur's heir.
Shelob is a giant spider that dwells in the pass of Cirith Ungol.
Shelob stung Frodo, and Sam fought her off with the Phial of Galadriel and Sting.
Galadriel is the Lady of Lothlórien.
Galadriel gave Frodo the Phial of Galadriel as a gift.
Galadriel refused the One Ring when Frodo offered it to her.
The One Ring was forged by Sauron in the fires of Mount Doom.
The One Ring can only be destroyed in the fires of Mount Doom where it was made.
Sauron lost the One Ring when Isildur cut it from his hand.
Isildur kept the One Ring rather than destroying it.
Isildur was killed by orcs at the Gladden Fields and the Ring was lost in the river.
The Nazgûl are nine Men enslaved by Sauron through the nine Rings of Power.
The Witch-king of Angmar is the leader of the Nazgûl.
Three Rings of Power were made for the Elves.
Seven Rings of Power were made for the Dwarf-lords.
Nine Rings of Power were made for mortal Men.
Elrond held the Elven ring Vilya.
Galadriel held the Elven ring Nenya.
Gandalf bore the Elven ring Narya, given to him by Círdan the Shipwright.
Bilbo celebrated his one hundred and eleventh birthday at the beginning of The Lord of the Rings.
Bilbo gave the One Ring to Frodo before departing for Rivendell.
Bilbo wrote a book called There and Back Again about his adventures.
Thorin Oakenshield led a company of thirteen dwarves to reclaim the Lonely Mountain.
Thorin died from wounds received in the Battle of Five Armies.
Fíli and Kíli also died in the Battle of Five Armies.
Bard the Bowman killed Smaug with a black arrow.
Smaug attacked Lake-town after Bilbo's intrusion into his lair.
Beorn is a skin-changer who can take the form of a great bear.
The Arkenstone was the most prized treasure of the dwarves of Erebor.
Bilbo secretly gave the Arkenstone to Bard and Thranduil to use as a bargaining tool.
Thorin nearly killed Bilbo over the theft of the Arkenstone.
The trolls that captured Bilbo's company were turned to stone by sunlight.
Gandalf tricked the trolls into arguing until dawn broke and petrified them.
Bilbo won the riddle game against Gollum in the goblin tunnels.
Bilbo's final riddle to Gollum was asking what he had in his pocket.
Sting is an Elven blade that glows blue in the presence of orcs.
Bilbo found Sting in the trolls' cave.
Glamdring was also found in the trolls' cave and was carried by Gandalf.
The Great Eagles rescued Thorin's company from orcs and wargs in the Misty Mountains.
Mirkwood was once called Greenwood the Great before Sauron's shadow fell upon it.
Thranduil is the Elvenking who imprisoned Thorin's company in his halls in Mirkwood.
The dwarves escaped Thranduil's halls by hiding in barrels floated down the river.
Lake-town, also called Esgaroth, was a settlement of Men built upon the Long Lake.
The Battle of Five Armies was fought between dwarves, elves, men, orcs, and wargs.
Dáin Ironfoot became King under the Mountain after Thorin's death.
Gandalf carried the sword Glamdring, which was forged in Gondolin.
Minas Tirith is the capital city of Gondor.
Minas Morgul was once called Minas Ithil before it fell to the Nazgûl.
Osgiliath was the ancient capital of Gondor, situated on the river Anduin.
Denethor was the Steward of Gondor during the War of the Ring.
Denethor burned himself alive on a funeral pyre in madness and despair.
Faramir is the younger son of Denethor and brother of Boromir.
Faramir allowed Frodo and Sam to continue their quest rather than seizing the Ring.
Pippin looked into the palantír of Orthanc and was seen by Sauron.
Aragorn used the palantír of Orthanc to reveal himself to Sauron as the heir of Isildur.
Aragorn wielded the reforged sword Andúril, which was remade from the shards of Narsil.
Narsil was the sword of Elendil that broke when he fell fighting Sauron.
Aragorn married Arwen Undómiel after being crowned King of Gondor.
Arwen chose a mortal life to be with Aragorn, giving up her Elven immortality.
Elrond is the father of Arwen.
The Paths of the Dead pass under the White Mountains.
Helm's Deep is a fortress in Rohan where a great battle was fought against Saruman's forces.
Gríma Wormtongue served as Saruman's spy in the court of King Théoden.
Gandalf freed Théoden from Saruman's influence at Edoras.
Saruman was killed by Gríma Wormtongue in the Shire.
The Scouring of the Shire occurred when the hobbits returned home after the War of the Ring.
Lothlórien is a golden forest protected by the power of Galadriel.
The Mirror of Galadriel shows visions of past, present, and possible future events.
Sam saw the destruction of the Shire in the Mirror of Galadriel.
The river Anduin is the great river that flows through much of western Middle-earth.
The Argonath are two great statues of Isildur and Anárion standing upon the river Anduin.
Gollum's real name is Sméagol.
Gollum fell into the fires of Mount Doom while holding the One Ring, destroying it.
Gollum bit the Ring from Frodo's finger at the Crack of Doom.
Frodo claimed the One Ring for himself at the Crack of Doom and refused to destroy it.
Tom Bombadil is unaffected by the power of the One Ring.
The One Ring had no effect on Tom Bombadil when he wore it.
The Old Forest lies east of the Shire and is home to Tom Bombadil.
Weathertop is the hill where Frodo was stabbed by the Witch-king with a Morgul-blade.
Frodo bore the wound from the Morgul-blade for the rest of his time in Middle-earth.
Frodo sailed from the Grey Havens to the Undying Lands at the end of the Third Age.
Bilbo also sailed to the Undying Lands with Frodo, Gandalf, and the Ring-bearers.
Gandalf sailed to the Undying Lands from the Grey Havens after the War of the Ring.
Sam eventually became Mayor of the Shire.
Sam married Rosie Cotton after returning to the Shire.
The Prancing Pony is an inn in the town of Bree where Frodo first met Aragorn.
Aragorn was known as Strider among the people of Bree.
The Council of Elrond was held in Rivendell to decide the fate of the One Ring.
The decision at the Council of Elrond was to destroy the One Ring in Mount Doom.
Mordor is the dark land ruled by Sauron in the southeast of Middle-earth.
The Black Gate of Mordor is called the Morannon.
Aragorn led an army to the Black Gate to distract Sauron while Frodo entered Mordor.
Sauron's power was broken when the One Ring was destroyed in Mount Doom.
Barad-dûr is the Dark Tower of Sauron in Mordor.
The palantíri are ancient seeing-stones that allow communication across great distances.

era_false.statement: Gandalf was originally a mortal man who learned magic through years of study in Gondor.
Frodo destroyed the One Ring by casting it into Mount Doom of his own free will.
Sauron forged the One Ring in the mines of Moria.
Aragorn was the last surviving member of the House of Fëanor.
Legolas was the son of Celeborn, Lord of Lothlórien.
Gimli refused to enter the Glittering Caves of Helm's Deep out of fear.
Samwise Gamgee betrayed Frodo on the stairs of Cirith Ungol.
Boromir survived the breaking of the Fellowship and returned to Minas Tirith.
The Shire was located on the eastern side of the Misty Mountains.
Saruman was one of the nine Nazgûl who served Sauron.
Gollum willingly gave the One Ring to Bilbo during their riddle game.
Théoden was the king of Gondor during the War of the Ring.
Treebeard and the Ents refused to march against Isengard.
Éowyn slew the Witch-king of Angmar using an Elvish blade from Rivendell.
Merry and Pippin were captured by Elves during the breaking of the Fellowship.
The Balrog of Moria was a servant of Saruman sent to stop the Fellowship.
Arwen sailed to the Undying Lands instead of marrying Aragorn.
Bilbo found the One Ring inside the trolls' cave in The Hobbit.
Gandalf the White was more powerful than Gandalf the Grey because he absorbed Saruman's power.
The Dead Marshes were located directly south of Rivendell.
Denethor was a wise and just steward who welcomed Aragorn as king.
Elrond forged the shards of Narsil into Andúril before the Council of Elrond.
Smaug was slain by Thorin Oakenshield in single combat inside the Lonely Mountain.
The palantíri were created by Sauron to corrupt the leaders of Men.
Shelob was the offspring of Smaug and dwelt in the tunnels beneath Mordor.
Faramir immediately tried to take the One Ring from Frodo when they met.
Galadriel accepted the One Ring when Frodo offered it to her in Lothlórien.
Rohan was a kingdom of Elves allied with Gondor.
The Witch-king of Angmar wielded one of the three Elven Rings of Power.
Tom Bombadil was deeply affected by the power of the One Ring.
Isildur destroyed the One Ring after cutting it from Sauron's hand.
Wormtongue killed Saruman during the Battle of Helm's Deep.
The Fellowship of the Ring consisted of seven members chosen at the Council of Elrond.
Lothlórien was a Dwarvish kingdom hidden deep underground.
Gandalf rode the eagle Gwaihir into the Battle of the Black Gate to fight Sauron directly.
The Ents were created by Aulë the Smith, the same Vala who created the Dwarves.
Pippin looked into the palantír of Orthanc and saw a vision of the Shire's destruction.
Aragorn was raised in Lothlórien under the care of Galadriel and Celeborn.
The One Ring could only be worn by members of the race of Men.
Sauron appeared in physical form during the Battle of the Black Gate.
Minas Morgul was the former capital of Rohan before Sauron conquered it.
Legolas and Gimli were bitter enemies throughout the entire quest.
The Nazgûl were former Elven lords corrupted by Sauron's rings.
Gandalf defeated the Balrog in Moria without dying in the process.
Bilbo attended the Council of Elrond and volunteered to carry the Ring to Mordor.
The Eagles refused to help during the Battle of Five Armies.
Thorin Oakenshield survived the Battle of Five Armies and ruled as King under the Mountain for decades.
Gollum possessed the One Ring for approximately fifty years before losing it to Bilbo.
Rivendell was founded by Galadriel as a refuge for the Elves of Middle-earth.
The Mouth of Sauron was a fallen Wizard, one of the Istari.
Frodo was Bilbo's nephew by blood relation.
Helm's Deep was a fortress built by the Elves of the First Age.
The river Anduin flowed from south to north through Middle-earth.
Saruman bred the Uruk-hai by crossing Orcs with Trolls.
Shadowfax was a black horse that Gandalf stole from Théoden's stables.
The Mirror of Galadriel always showed events that would certainly come to pass.
Merry killed the Witch-king of Angmar without any help from Éowyn.
Elrond was a full-blooded Elf with no mortal ancestry.
The Dwarves of Erebor sent a large army to aid Gondor at Minas Tirith.
Frodo put on the One Ring during the Council of Elrond to demonstrate its power.
Sauron needed the One Ring to maintain his physical form.
Gandalf used the One Ring briefly to defeat the Balrog in Khazad-dûm.
The Prancing Pony inn was located in the town of Bree in the land of Rohan.
Aragorn broke his sword Andúril during the Battle of the Pelennor Fields.
Bill the Pony carried the hobbits all the way to Mordor.
The Entwives were found living peacefully in Fangorn Forest.
Saruman's tower of Orthanc was destroyed by the Ents during their attack on Isengard.
Frodo was the eldest of the four hobbits who left the Shire together.
Gollum fell into Mount Doom because Frodo pushed him off the edge.
The Grey Havens were a port city ruled by Círdan the Shipwright in eastern Middle-earth.
Legolas was over ten thousand years old during the War of the Ring.
Denethor died in battle defending the gates of Minas Tirith.
The Ringwraiths could see perfectly well in daylight but were blind in darkness.
Gandalf carried the Elven Ring Vilya, the Ring of Air.
Bard the Bowman killed Smaug with a regular arrow from a standard longbow.
The hobbits of the Shire had never seen fireworks before Gandalf arrived for Bilbo's party.
Minas Tirith had five levels rising up the mountainside.
Gollum guided Frodo and Sam through the Mines of Moria.
Elrond wanted Arwen to marry Aragorn and encouraged their relationship from the beginning.
The Dead Men of Dunharrow fought in the Battle of the Pelennor Fields by killing the enemy soldiers.
Saruman was the head of the White Council because he was the wisest of the Istari.
The One Ring grew heavier the closer it came to Rivendell.
Bilbo used the One Ring to become invisible during his speech at his eleventy-first birthday party and never returned.
Théoden died of old age after the War of the Ring ended peacefully.
Frodo and Sam crossed into Mordor through the Black Gate of Morannon.
Gimli was given a single hair from Galadriel's head as a parting gift in Lothlórien.
The Uruk-hai were vulnerable to sunlight just like ordinary Orcs.
Gandalf arrived in Middle-earth during the First Age to fight against Morgoth.
Radagast the Brown accompanied the Fellowship on their journey south from Rivendell.
The One Ring was inscribed with Elvish script written in the Quenya language.
Sauron's physical form was destroyed at the Battle of the Last Alliance by Gil-galad alone.
The Shire was protected by a powerful magical barrier erected by Gandalf.
Aragorn refused the throne of Gondor after the defeat of Sauron.
Beorn was a Dwarf who could transform into a bear through dark magic.
The Fellowship traveled through the Mines of Moria because Gandalf recommended it as the safest route.
Bilbo wrote the Red Book of Westmarch entirely by himself.
The Witch-king of Angmar was killed by a wound from Aragorn's sword at Weathertop.
Galadriel sailed to the Undying Lands before the War of the Ring began.
The three Elven Rings lost all power before the One Ring was destroyed.
Sméagol murdered his brother Déagol to obtain the One Ring.
Fangorn Forest was located on the eastern border of Mordor.
Wormtongue killed Saruman at the Battle of Bywater in the Shire.
The Corsairs of Umbar were Dark Elves who served Sauron willingly.
Gandalf refused to touch or carry the One Ring at any point during the story.
Bilbo won the riddle game against Gollum by answering all of Gollum's riddles correctly.
The sword Sting glowed red in the presence of Orcs.
Frodo was thirty-three years old when he left the Shire with the One Ring.
Elrond participated in the Last Alliance and personally witnessed Isildur take the One Ring.
Merry and Pippin grew taller after drinking water from the River Anduin.
The Istari were Elves sent by the Valar to guide the peoples of Middle-earth.
Sauron could see everything that happened in Middle-earth through the palantír of Barad-dûr.
Aragorn and Arwen had three children after the War of the Ring.
The Balrog of Moria had been awakened by Sauron's growing power.
Lothlórien's golden trees were called mallorn, and they grew abundantly throughout Middle-earth.
Bilbo gave Frodo his mithril coat at the Grey Havens as a farewell gift.
The kingdom of Dale was destroyed permanently by Smaug and never rebuilt.
Gandalf was the only Wizard who remained faithful to his original mission in Middle-earth.
Théoden charged into the Battle of the Pelennor Fields after being freed from Saruman's control by Aragorn.
The One Ring could be destroyed by any sufficiently hot dragon fire.
Frodo offered to give the One Ring to Gandalf, and Gandalf accepted it temporarily for safekeeping.

modern_true.statement: Water freezes at zero degrees Celsius at standard atmospheric pressure.
The Earth orbits the Sun once every 365.25 days.
DNA carries the genetic instructions for all known living organisms.
The Moon causes tides on Earth through gravitational pull.
Light travels at 299,792 kilometers per second in a vacuum.
The human body contains 206 bones in adulthood.
Antibiotics kill bacteria but have no effect on viruses.
The Pacific Ocean is the largest ocean on Earth.
Mount Everest stands 8,849 meters above sea level.
The Amazon River is the largest river by water volume.
Australia is both a country and a continent.
The Great Wall of China stretches over 21,000 kilometers.
Diamonds are composed of carbon atoms arranged in a crystal lattice.
The human heart beats over 100,000 times per day.
Electricity flows through copper more easily than through rubber.
The Sahara Desert is the largest hot desert on Earth.
Gravity on the Moon is one-sixth of Earth's gravity.
Oxygen makes up 21 percent of Earth's atmosphere by volume.
The speed of sound in air is 343 meters per second.
Photosynthesis converts carbon dioxide and water into glucose and oxygen.
The Nile River flows through eleven African countries.
Jupiter is the largest planet in the Solar System.
Penicillin was discovered by Alexander Fleming in 1928.
The atomic number of hydrogen is one.
The Berlin Wall fell on November 9, 1989.
World War II ended in 1945 with the surrender of Japan.
The French Revolution began in 1789.
The printing press was invented by Johannes Gutenberg around 1440.
Neil Armstrong walked on the Moon on July 20, 1969.
The United States declared independence from Britain in 1776.
The Titanic sank in the North Atlantic Ocean on April 15, 1912.
The Roman Empire fell in 476 AD.
Charles Darwin published On the Origin of Species in 1859.
The Wright brothers achieved powered flight at Kitty Hawk in 1903.
The Industrial Revolution began in Britain in the late 18th century.
Mahatma Gandhi led India's nonviolent independence movement against British rule.
The atomic bomb was dropped on Hiroshima on August 6, 1945.
The Soviet Union dissolved in December 1991.
The Panama Canal connects the Atlantic and Pacific Oceans.
Napoleon Bonaparte was exiled to the island of Saint Helena in 1815.
The Black Death killed an estimated one-third of Europe's population in the 14th century.
Julius Caesar was assassinated on March 15, 44 BC.
The first successful heart transplant was performed by Christiaan Barnard in 1967.
China has the largest population of any single country on Earth.
Tokyo is the most populous metropolitan area in the world.
Russia is the largest country by land area.
Vatican City is the smallest independent country in the world.
The Mariana Trench is the deepest point in the ocean.
The Danube River flows through ten European countries.
Indonesia consists of more than 17,000 islands.
Canada has the longest coastline of any country.
Lake Baikal in Russia is the deepest freshwater lake on Earth.
The Andes is the longest continental mountain range in the world.
Iceland sits on the Mid-Atlantic Ridge between two tectonic plates.
Madagascar is the fourth-largest island in the world.
The Dead Sea is the lowest point on Earth's land surface.
Bangladesh is one of the most densely populated countries in the world.
The Himalayas were formed by the collision of the Indian and Eurasian tectonic plates.
Brazil is the largest country in South America by area and population.
The Strait of Gibraltar separates Europe from Africa.
Earth's inner core is composed primarily of iron and nickel.
Transistors are the fundamental building blocks of modern computer processors.
The Internet was developed from ARPANET, a U.S. military research network.
Vaccines stimulate the immune system to produce antibodies against specific pathogens.
Nuclear fusion powers the Sun by converting hydrogen into helium.
Plate tectonics explains the movement of Earth's lithospheric plates.
Insulin regulates blood sugar levels in the human body.
The double helix structure of DNA was described by Watson and Crick in 1953.
Radio waves, visible light, and X-rays are all forms of electromagnetic radiation.
The periodic table organizes chemical elements by atomic number and properties.
Black holes have gravitational fields so strong that light cannot escape them.
The human genome contains over three billion base pairs of DNA.
Neurons transmit electrical signals through the human nervous system.
Absolute zero is minus 273.15 degrees Celsius.
The Hubble Space Telescope was launched into orbit in 1990.
Tectonic plate movement causes earthquakes along fault lines.
Helium is the second most abundant element in the observable universe.
Sound cannot travel through a vacuum.
The Milky Way galaxy contains over 100 billion stars.
Magnetism and electricity are two aspects of the same fundamental force.
The Spanish Inquisition was established in 1478 by Ferdinand and Isabella.
The Rosetta Stone was discovered by French soldiers in Egypt in 1799.
The Renaissance began in Italy during the 14th century.
The Suez Canal opened for navigation in 1869.
Genghis Khan founded the Mongol Empire in 1206.
The first photograph was taken by Joseph Nicéphore Niépce in 1826.
Martin Luther King Jr. delivered his "I Have a Dream" speech in 1963.
The Chernobyl nuclear disaster occurred in Ukraine on April 26, 1986.
The Universal Declaration of Human Rights was adopted by the United Nations in 1948.
The Ottoman Empire lasted from 1299 to 1922.
Slavery was abolished in the United States by the Thirteenth Amendment in 1865.
The first human-made satellite, Sputnik 1, was launched by the Soviet Union in 1957.
The Magna Carta was sealed in England in 1215.
The Korean War began in 1950 and ended with an armistice in 1953.
William Shakespeare wrote his plays in Elizabethan England during the late 16th century.
The Voyager 1 spacecraft has traveled farther from Earth than any other human-made object.
Coral reefs are built by colonies of tiny living organisms called coral polyps.
The human brain contains roughly 86 billion neurons.
Steel is an alloy made primarily of iron and carbon.
The equator divides the Earth into the Northern and Southern Hemispheres.
Arabic numerals originated in India before spreading to the Arab world and Europe.
The speed of Earth's rotation causes the Coriolis effect on weather patterns.
Chlorophyll gives plants their green color by absorbing red and blue light.
The Galápagos Islands are located in the Pacific Ocean west of Ecuador.
The Great Barrier Reef lies off the northeast coast of Australia.
Sanskrit is one of the oldest known languages with a written record.
Chess originated in India before the 7th century AD.
Coffee was first cultivated in Ethiopia.
The human eye can distinguish over ten million different colors.
The Eiffel Tower was completed in Paris in 1889.
Mars has two moons named Phobos and Deimos.
Gold is chemically inert and does not rust or corrode under normal conditions.
The Antarctic ice sheet contains 70 percent of Earth's fresh water.
The first programmable electronic computer, ENIAC, was completed in 1945.
Bacteria outnumber human cells in the human body.
The Caspian Sea is the largest enclosed body of water on Earth.
Saturn's rings are composed primarily of ice particles and rocky debris.
The human body is composed of roughly 60 percent water by mass.
The Renaissance artist Leonardo da Vinci painted the Mona Lisa.
Volcanic eruptions release gases including water vapor, carbon dioxide, and sulfur dioxide.
